coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy 5–6 miles, Z2 (130–140 bpm), minimal vert. You're in week 20 taper and your resting HR dropped to 53 bpm—your body is adapting well. This is a shakeout before tomorrow's long run; keep it conversational, sub-7:30/mi pace. Skip any tempo or hill work. You've already logged 33 miles this week; volume is down ~32% vs. last week, which is exactly right for taper. Focus on leg turnover and mental readiness, not intensity.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Eat 200–250g carbs today distributed across breakfast, lunch, snack, and dinner. Your sleep jumped to 6.54 hours (88% efficiency) and your 30-day average is still only 5.13 hours—you're catching up, which is good, but tomorrow's long run demands full glycogen stores. Hydrate aggressively: aim for 3–4L by evening. Skip any fasted running; eat a light breakfast (bagel + honey + coffee) 90 minutes before the run.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Recovery score is 73/100—solid. No HRV data available, but your RHR trend (down from 61 to 53 bpm) signals strong parasympathetic recovery. Target 7+ hours tonight; you have a long run tomorrow and you're banking sleep debt from travel. Use an ice bath or contrast shower after the run if available; 10 min total is enough. Foam roll calves and quads for 5 minutes before bed—alpine descents will hammer your quads in Slovenia.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 98 days out, in controlled taper, and your body is responding. This is the phase where doubt creeps in—the workouts feel easy and your brain starts second-guessing readiness. Resist that. Julian Alps has 2,500m of vert on sparse aid; technique and pacing discipline matter more than max fitness. Use today's shakeout to lock in your descending form and practice breathing under control. Tomorrow you'll test your long-run fueling strategy. Trust the process—you've earned this.
